metadata.agency: New York State Office of Temporary and Disability Assistance
metadata.rulemaking: Notice of Emergency Adoption and Proposed Rule Making

body: (a) The standard allowance for heating/cooling consists of costs for heating and air conditioning. The standard allowance for heating/cooling is available to households which incur or anticipate incurring heating or cooling costs separately and apart from their rent or mortgage, or to households with an elderly or disabled member entitled to a HEAP or LIHEAA payment. Households in public housing or rental housing units with central utility meters who are charged only for excess heating or cooling costs are not entitled to this standard allowance unless they received such a payment. As of October 1, [2024] 2025, but subject to subsequent adjustments as required by the USDA, the standard allowance for heating/cooling for SNAP applicant and recipient households residing in New York City is [$1,034] $1,062; for households residing in either Suffolk or Nassau Counties, it is [$962] $988; and for households residing in any other county of New York State, it is [$854] $877.
heading: Standard allowance for heating/cooling

metadata.agency: New York State Office of Temporary and Disability Assistance
metadata.rulemaking: Notice of Emergency Adoption and Proposed Rule Making

body: (b) The standard allowance for utilities consists of costs for electricity not used to heat or cool the residence; cooking fuel; the cost of sewage, trash collection and water; the basic service fee for one telephone; and fees charged by a utility provider for initial installation of utilities. The standard allowance for utilities is available to households which do not qualify for the standard allowance for heating/cooling and which are billed separately from their rent or mortgage for one or more of these utilities other than telephone. As of October 1, [2024] 2025, but subject to subsequent adjustments as required by the USDA, the standard allowance for utilities for SNAP applicant and recipient households residing in New York City is [$407] $419; for households residing in either Suffolk or Nassau Counties, it is [$378] $388; and for households residing in any other county of New York State, it is [$346] $355.
heading: Standard allowance for utilities

metadata.agency: New York State Office of Temporary and Disability Assistance
metadata.rulemaking: Notice of Emergency Adoption and Proposed Rule Making

body: (c) The standard allowance for telephone consists of the cost for basic service for one telephone. The standard allowance for telephone is available to households which do not qualify for the standard allowance for heating/cooling or the standard allowance for utilities. As of October 1, [2024] 2025, but subject to subsequent adjustments as required by the USDA, the standard allowance for telephone for all SNAP applicant and recipient households residing in New York State is [$31] $32.
heading: Standard allowance for telephone
